items.title: No injuries after explosion reported near U.S. Embassy in Norway, local police say
items.standfirst: Norwegian police reported on Sunday an explosion near the U.S. Embassy in the capital Oslo, but said there were no casualties.
items.content: Norwegian police reported on Sunday an explosion near the U.S. Embassy in the capital Oslo, but said there were no casualties.

items.title: No injuries reported after explosion at U.S. Embassy in Oslo, police say
items.standfirst: An explosion at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, Norway, on Saturday caused minor damage but no injuries, police in the Scandinavian nation said.
items.content: An explosion at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, Norway, on Saturday caused minor damage but no injuries, police in the Scandinavian nation said.

items.title: Loud explosion rocks US embassy in Oslo, police search for perpetrators
items.standfirst: A noisy explosion hit the entrance of the US embassy in Oslo early Sunday, shattering glass but causing no injuries, Norwegian police said. While the cause of the blast was unknown, Oslo police commander Michael Dellemyr told local media it appeared that the act had been carried out by someone.
items.content: A noisy explosion hit the entrance of the US embassy in Oslo early Sunday, shattering glass but causing no injuries, Norwegian police said. While the cause of the blast was unknown, Oslo police commander Michael Dellemyr told local media it appeared that the act had been carried out by someone.

ai_summary: Explosion occurred at US embassy in Oslo Norway
Minor damage reported but no injuries
Norwegian police investigating the incident

No casualties reported by local authorities

items.title: Trump convenes ‘Shield of Americas’ summit with 12 Latin American leaders
items.standfirst: In Miami, president calls for regional cooperation to counter Chinese economic and political interestsDonald Trump changed the channel from Iran to the western hemisphere on Saturday, convening a gathering of Latin American leaders at his Miami-area golf club to discuss regional interests and establishing what he called a “counter-cartel coalition”.“Just as we formed a coalition to eradicate Isis, we now need a coalition to eradicate the cartels,” he told 12 regional leaders gathered at what the White House called the “Shield of the Americas” summit. Continue reading...

items.content: US President Donald Trump encouraged right-wing Latin American leaders gathered at his Florida golf club on Saturday to take military action against drug trafficking cartels and transnational gangs that he says pose an “unacceptable threat” to the region's national security.

items.title: Iran fires at Gulf neighbours as president’s apology draws criticism at home
items.standfirst: Iran's president apologised on Saturday for attacks on "neighbouring countries" even as its missiles and drones continued to target US bases in Gulf Arab states and hardliners in Tehran asserted that the country's war strategy would not budge. Iran also traded blows with Israel, whose Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pleged to pursue the war "with all our force" and to "eradicate the Iranian regime".
items.content: Iran's president apologised on Saturday for attacks on "neighbouring countries" even as its missiles and drones continued to target US bases in Gulf Arab states and hardliners in Tehran asserted that the country's war strategy would not budge. Iran also traded blows with Israel, whose Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pleged to pursue the war "with all our force" and to "eradicate the Iranian regime".

items.title: Iran rejects Trump’s demand for unconditional surrender as a ‘dream’
items.standfirst: Masoud Pezeshkian issues rare apology to neighbouring Gulf states for Iranian strikes as war enters eighth day Middle East crisis live: latest news updatesThe president of Iran has rejected Donald Trump’s call for the country’s unconditional surrender as a “dream”, while issuing a rare apology for Iranian attacks that hit neighbouring states, even as missiles and drones continued to strike Gulf countries.In a prerecorded address broadcast on state television on Saturday, Iran’s president, Masoud Pezeshkian, said the country would never capitulate, responding to remarks by the US president, who said on Friday that only Iran’s total submission could bring the war to an end. Continue reading...
items.content: Masoud Pezeshkian issues rare apology to neighbouring Gulf states for Iranian strikes as war enters eighth day Middle East crisis live: latest news updatesThe president of Iran has rejected Donald Trump’s call for the country’s unconditional surrender as a “dream”, while issuing a rare apology for Iranian attacks that hit neighbouring states, even as missiles and drones continued to strike Gulf countries.In a prerecorded address broadcast on state television on Saturday, Iran’s president, Masoud Pezeshkian, said the country would never capitulate, responding to remarks by the US president, who said on Friday that only Iran’s total submission could bring the war to an end. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Israel launched wave of strikes against Iranian military sites
Iran rejects Trump's demand for unconditional surrender
Iranian president apologizes for attacks on neighboring countries
Israel vows to pursue Iranian regime with all force
Conflict between Israel and Iran continues to escalate

clusters.neutral_headline: US Agency Fails to Perform Safety Checks

items.title: Why weren't safety checks done for 100+ food additives? #health
items.standfirst: Regulatory gaps allowed unvetted ingredients into the food supply A review of government records found that more than 100 substances now in common use in U.S. foods did not undergo complete safety assessments by the relevant federal agency. The lapse stems from loopholes in the regulatory…
items.content: Regulatory gaps allowed unvetted ingredients into the food supply A review of government records found that more than 100 substances now in common use in U.S. foods did not undergo complete safety assessments by the relevant federal agency. The lapse stems from loopholes in the regulatory…

items.title: US agency did not perform safety checks of more than 100 food ingredients, analysis finds
items.standfirst: Review of FDA records by the Environmental Working Group reveals firms are exploiting rule to send new chemicals in food systemMore than 100 substances widely used in common US foods, supplements and beverages underwent no health and safety review by the US Food and Drug Administration, a new analysis of federal records finds.The review of FDA records by the Environmental Working Group (EWG) non-profit reveals that diverse products across the food pyramid, such as Capri Sun drinks, Kettle and Fire organic broth, Acme smoked fish, and Quaker Oats snack bars, use a range of substances that have not undergone review by regulators. Continue reading...
items.content: Review of FDA records by the Environmental Working Group reveals firms are exploiting rule to send new chemicals in food systemMore than 100 substances widely used in common US foods, supplements and beverages underwent no health and safety review by the US Food and Drug Administration, a new analysis of federal records finds.The review of FDA records by the Environmental Working Group (EWG) non-profit reveals that diverse products across the food pyramid, such as Capri Sun drinks, Kettle and Fire organic broth, Acme smoked fish, and Quaker Oats snack bars, use a range of substances that have not undergone review by regulators. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Over 100 food additives not reviewed for safety
Regulatory gaps allowed unvetted ingredients into food supply
FDA records show lack of safety assessments
Loopholes in regulations contributed to lapse
Environmental Working Group reveals findings
